name: Licorice Caramels
recipeIngredient: 14 oz Can (1-1/4 cups) sweetened condensed milk
1/2 tsp Black or red coloring paste
recipeInstructions:
1. Line a 9 x 9 x 2 inch baking pan with foil, extending foil over the edges of the pan. Butter the foil; set aside.
2. In a heavy 3-quart saucepan, melt the butter over low heat. Add the sugar, sweetened condensed milk, light corn syrup, and salt; mix well.
3. Carefully clip a candy thermometer to the side of the pan.
4. Cook over medium heat, stirring frequently until the candy thermometer registers 244°F (firm-ball stage). The mixture should boil at a moderate, steady rate over its entire surface. Reaching firm-ball stage should take 15 to 20 minutes. (Mixture scorches easily.)
5. Remove from heat and remove the candy thermometer from the saucepan.
6. Add anise extract and coloring; stir to mix.
7. Quickly pour the candy into the buttered, foil-lined pan. Cool for several hours or until firm.
8. Using foil, lift the candy out of the pan onto a cutting board. Peel away the foil; discard.
9. With a buttered sharp knife, cut immediately into 1-inch squares.
10. To wrap caramels: Tear off 6-inch strips of waxed paper and cut each strip into 4-inch widths, making pieces 6 x 4 inches. Roll a caramel in a length of paper; fold flaps under. Set the caramels into gift boxes with flaps underneath. The caramels will pack neatly and stay tightly wrapped.
Makes 81 pieces (about 2-3/4 pounds)
